9947c9018992.plainText: What Is Shoulder Impingement? Shoulder impingement occurs when the tendons of the rotator cuff become compressed (or "pinched") between the bones of the shoulder during movement. This can lead to irritation, inflammation, and pain.

What Is Shoulder Impingement?

Shoulder impingement occurs when the tendons of the rotator cuff become compressed (or "pinched") between the bones of the shoulder during movement. This can lead to irritation, inflammation, and pain.

What Is Shoulder Impingement?

Shoulder impingement occurs when the tendons of the rotator cuff become compressed (or "pinched") between the bones of the shoulder during movement. This can lead to irritation, inflammation, and pain.

What Causes Shoulder Impingement?

This condition often develops gradually and is commonly related to

008ba9f13245.plainText: What Causes Shoulder Impingement? This condition often develops gradually and is commonly related to movement patterns and overuse. Common causes include: ✓ Repetitive overhead activities (sports, work, lifting) ✓ Poor posture (rounded shoulders) ✓ Muscle imbalances or weakness ✓ Tight shoulder structures ✓ Previous shoulder injuries
008ba9f13245.originalHtml:
What Causes Shoulder Impingement?

This condition often develops gradually and is commonly related to movement patterns and overuse.

Common causes include:

  • ✓ Repetitive overhead activities (sports, work, lifting)
  • ✓ Poor posture (rounded shoulders)
  • ✓ Muscle imbalances or weakness
  • ✓ Tight shoulder structures
  • ✓ Previous shoulder injuries
008ba9f13245.previewContext:
line-height:1.7">Shoulder impingement occurs when the tendons of the rotator cuff become compressed (or "pinched") between the bones of the shoulder during movement. This can lead to irritation, inflammation, and pain.

What Causes Shoulder Impingement?

This condition often develops gradually and is commonly related to movement patterns and overuse.

Common causes include:

  • ✓ Repetitive overhead activities (sports, work, lifting)
  • ✓ Poor posture (rounded shoulders)
  • ✓ Muscle imbalances or weakness
  • ✓ Tight shoulder structures
  • ✓ Previous shoulder injuries
